publicationStmt.publisher: Schenker Documents Online in association with the Centre for Computing in the Humanities

sourceDesc.p: No source: created in electronic format.

encodingDesc.p: Encoding according to the CCH TEI Guidelines

div.p: The second son of Reinhard Oppel and Gertrud Oppel (née Bandelow).

Walter was Reinhard Oppel’s fourth child, and the second of his second marriage, to Gertrude Bandelow, his siblings in that marriage being Ulrich and Hans Reinhard[entity-008510], all three boys being adversely affected by the death in 1925 of their mother Gertrud.

Walter did not flourish academically and left school in April 1930 aged 16 to attend business school, moving to Hamburg[entity-002589]-Altona in the September to start commercial training. By early 1933 he was employed and supporting himself. In late 1934 he was doing community service (Arbeitsdienst – probably involving Nazi party indoctrination). In October 1935 he was posted to an infantry regiment on the Baltic coast for one year, which he apparently enjoyed. He fought during World War II on the eastern front, was taken prisoner by the Russians, and died in captivity in Smolensk in 1947.

div.head: Walter Oppel and Schenker
div.p: Walter is frequently mentioned throughout Reinhard Oppel’s correspondence with Schenker. He himself occasionally signed a letter or postcard, calling himself “der Torpedo.” A photograph of him with his two siblings is preserved at OJ 72/11, item 4c (May 1921). Like Ulrich, he was keen on photography, helping Ulrich with developing and printing.
